title: Old Yeller Belly

jokes.text: Teacher hit me with a ruler / I cracked her in the bean / With a frozen Jimmy Dean / And she ain't my teacher no more / Because she's dead
jokes.setup: Parody of 'Battle Hymn of the Republic' suggesting typical school rebellion
jokes.punchline: Escalates to actual murder with frozen breakfast sausage as weapon
jokes.explanation: Dark humor escalation with perfect rhyme scheme and absurd weapon choice. Classic Simpsons subversion of innocent childhood song.

jokes.text: Girls are yucky. Seconded and carried. A fart was detected. Martin denied it...and so was ruled to have supplied it.
jokes.setup: Formal meeting minutes format for boys' treehouse club
jokes.punchline: Juxtaposition of parliamentary procedure with juvenile concerns
jokes.explanation: Solid structural comedy contrasting formal language with childish content. Well-executed but not groundbreaking.

jokes.text: These Batpants have been shredded by the Riddler. No, just your ass. That's what I call my ass.
jokes.setup: Martin seems to be role-playing as Batman with damaged costume
jokes.punchline: Reveals he just has a torn seat and refers to his butt as 'the Riddler'
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment for Martin - nerdy, imaginative, and slightly pathetic. The misdirection works well.

jokes.text: If I understand the female mind, and I do, they're plotting against us.
jokes.setup: Martin claims expertise about understanding females
jokes.punchline: The confidence of his claim combined with his obvious cluelessness
jokes.explanation: Standard nerd character comedy. The self-assurance makes it work for Martin specifically.

jokes.text: Each year we lose seven species of gumdrops
jokes.setup: Environmental awareness presentation about species loss
jokes.punchline: Treats candy as endangered wildlife
jokes.explanation: Clever environmental satire with absurdist twist. Economic and fits Lisa's character well.

jokes.text: [IMITATING MARGE] Lisa, this is your mother. The doctor says your butt's too big.
jokes.setup: Bart spying and trying to disrupt Lisa's tea party
jokes.punchline: Crude impersonation with inappropriate medical advice
jokes.explanation: Typical Bart mischief but relies on crude humor. Gets the job done but isn't particularly clever.

jokes.text: No one told me there'd be pulling.
jokes.setup: Boys' club gets into physical altercation with girls
jokes.punchline: Martin's complaint about the unexpected physicality
jokes.explanation: Perfect Martin line - whiny, literal, and cowardly. The understatement during chaos works well.

jokes.text: I'll build you a new tree house. One so grand...it'll be an affront to God himself.
jokes.setup: Homer promising to rebuild destroyed treehouse
jokes.punchline: Escalates promise to offending deity
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer grandiosity with religious irreverence. The escalation from 'grand' to 'affront to God' is perfectly Homer.

jokes.text: Only losers buy wood.
jokes.setup: Passing lumberyard while needing lumber
jokes.punchline: Homer's illogical reasoning for not shopping at appropriate store
jokes.explanation: Homer's anti-logic at its finest. Economical and character-specific reasoning that makes no sense.

jokes.text: This track has been abandoned since 19... Uh-oh.
jokes.setup: Homer assuring Bart the train track is safe because it's abandoned
jokes.punchline: Realizes mid-sentence that a train is coming
jokes.explanation: Classic setup/payoff structure with perfect timing. The interrupted reassurance is textbook comedy.

jokes.text: Nothing beats flying across the country on a train.
jokes.setup: Passenger on a train that just hit people
jokes.punchline: The oblivious positive comment about train travel
jokes.explanation: Dark irony but not particularly memorable or character-driven.

jokes.text: Did Frank Lloyd Wright have to deal with people like you? / I have no idea who Frank Lloyd Wright is. / You said his name two seconds ago. / I was just putting words together.
jokes.setup: Homer invokes famous architect in his defense
jokes.punchline: Immediately admits he doesn't know who that is and was just making noise
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer moment - pretentious reference immediately undercut by his own stupidity. The 'putting words together' line is genius.

jokes.text: Homer and Bart having a nail gun fight
jokes.setup: Father and son working on construction project
jokes.punchline: Escalates to dangerous weapon play treated casually
jokes.explanation: Visual gag showing Homer's poor parenting. Relies on shock value of dangerous behavior treated lightly.

jokes.text: Didst thou hear that, Isaac? / Aye. Someone needs the Amish. / To the Buggymobile!
jokes.setup: Emergency call for construction help
jokes.punchline: Amish as superheroes with buggy instead of Batmobile
jokes.explanation: Clever superhero parody adapted to Amish culture. The portmanteau 'Buggymobile' is particularly good.

jokes.text: Oh, the Amish are so industrious. Not like those shiftless Mennonites.
jokes.setup: Praising Amish work ethic
jokes.punchline: Making fine distinctions between similar religious groups
jokes.explanation: Homer making arbitrary judgments between similar groups. The specificity of 'shiftless' adds to the absurdity.

jokes.text: Roll them bones. Three craps.
jokes.setup: Just criticized Mennonites as shiftless
jokes.punchline: Cut immediately to Mennonites gambling
jokes.explanation: Perfect immediate visual callback that proves Marge's prejudice right through comedic timing.

jokes.text: It really restores your faith in helpful weirdoes.
jokes.setup: Watching Amish construction work
jokes.punchline: Backhanded compliment calling them 'helpful weirdoes'
jokes.explanation: Homer's casual prejudice delivered as folk wisdom. The oxymoron of 'helpful weirdoes' is perfectly Homer.

jokes.text: Who knows more about electricity than the Amish?
jokes.setup: Praising Amish electrical work
jokes.punchline: Amish are famously opposed to modern technology including electricity
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup for electrical disaster. Homer's ignorance creates dramatic irony that pays off immediately.

jokes.text: By going down the ladder, you're agreeing not to sue.
jokes.setup: Tree house is on fire, people need to evacuate
jokes.punchline: Homer thinks about liability during emergency
jokes.explanation: Homer's misplaced priorities during crisis. Satirizes American litigation culture while showing his selfishness.

jokes.text: By going down the ladder, you're agreeing not to sue.
jokes.setup: People are escaping a fire
jokes.punchline: Homer's legalistic thinking even in an emergency
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist moment - Homer's priorities are completely backwards but perfectly in character.

jokes.text: By going down the ladder, you're agreeing not to sue.
jokes.setup: Treehouse on fire, people need to escape
jokes.punchline: Homer concerned about legal liability during emergency
jokes.explanation: Dark comedy gold - Homer's priorities are completely wrong during disaster. The legalistic thinking is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: Head burning. Legs freezing. Middle very pleasant.
jokes.setup: Homer stuck in burning treehouse
jokes.punchline: Detailed analysis of his uncomfortable situation with one positive note
jokes.explanation: Homer finding silver lining in disaster. The three-part structure and unexpected positivity make it memorable.

jokes.text: Pointy, pointy, spiky, stylish... Aah! Where's baldy?
jokes.setup: Marge doing head count of family members
jokes.punchline: Describes each family member by hair, calls Homer 'baldy'
jokes.explanation: Efficient character identification system. The rhythm and Marge's practical approach make it work.

jokes.text: Flames? Searing pain? A black cat? I must be in heaven.
jokes.setup: Homer experiencing obvious signs of distress and danger
jokes.punchline: Interprets hellish conditions as heavenly
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic - complete misinterpretation of reality. The escalating list builds well to the wrong conclusion.

jokes.text: I always thought cats were just for losers who live in apartments...until my life was saved by this sweet little kitty.
jokes.setup: Homer's previous prejudice against cats and cat owners
jokes.punchline: Complete reversal of opinion due to personal experience
jokes.explanation: Homer's flip-flopping personality on display. The apartment dweller insult feels authentic to his character.

jokes.text: I make you lasagna. You eat it up like the cat in the funny papers...who's not so funny.
jokes.setup: Italian chef praising the heroic cat
jokes.punchline: References Garfield but insults the comic strip
jokes.explanation: Luigi's broken English delivery with unexpected cultural criticism. The Garfield reference dates it but works for the character.

jokes.text: Today I can truly say Ich bin ein feline.
jokes.setup: Mayor giving speech about heroic cat
jokes.punchline: Parodies JFK's famous Berlin speech with cat pun
jokes.explanation: Clever historical reference with wordplay. The pun on 'Berliner' to 'feline' shows good craft and fits Quimby's pompous style.

jokes.text: Damn, that's a lemony cola.
jokes.setup: Mayor renames park with corporate sponsorship by Buzz Cola with Lemon
jokes.punchline: Immediately tries the product and gives unsolicited endorsement
jokes.explanation: Satirizes corporate sponsorship and politician corruption. Quimby's immediate product endorsement feels natural for his character.

jokes.text: I prefer catsup to ketchup. And to me, Yusuf Islam will always be Cat Stevens.
jokes.setup: Reporter asking about Homer being a cat person
jokes.punchline: Two strained connections to prove cat loyalty
jokes.explanation: Homer trying too hard to prove a point with weak connections. The Cat Stevens reference shows surprising cultural awareness for Homer.

jokes.text: God, no. It sucks.
jokes.setup: Reporter assumes Homer loves the musical Cats
jokes.punchline: Homer's blunt, immediate dismissal
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer honesty cutting through politeness. Economy is excellent - just three words that completely deflate expectations.

jokes.text: He was the first openly gay dog in Hollywood.
jokes.setup: Lisa praising Rin Tin Tin as role model for Santa's Little Helper
jokes.punchline: Applies modern identity politics to 1920s dog actor
jokes.explanation: Absurd application of identity politics to classic Hollywood. The anachronism and specificity make it memorable.

jokes.text: He bit me right in the Axis!
jokes.setup: Rin Tin Tin movie showing dog attacking Nazi
jokes.punchline: Nazi refers to his rear end as 'the Axis' (Axis powers)
jokes.explanation: Clever historical pun using WWII terminology. The specificity of the reference and physical comedy combination works well.

jokes.text: 'Grow a mustache,' says the wife. 'You'll look handsome,' says the wife.
jokes.setup: Mailman being attacked by dog
jokes.punchline: Blames facial hair for dog attack, suggested by wife
jokes.explanation: Classic hen-pecked husband comedy. The repetitive structure and misplaced blame work for the character moment.

jokes.text: Marge, prepare the emergency ham.
jokes.setup: Homer wants to torment the dog with food
jokes.punchline: Implies they have a ham specifically designated for emergencies
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer absurdity - the specificity of 'emergency ham' suggests a whole system of categorized food. Highly quotable.

jokes.text: He's young, he's slim, and he can stand on his hind legs. Unlike our current spokesman.
jokes.setup: Praising dog's qualifications for beer spokesperson
jokes.punchline: Implies Duffman can't stand up (presumably drunk)
jokes.explanation: Good setup comparing dog to human spokesman. The implied alcoholism of beer mascot is appropriately dark.

jokes.text: But what about my children? Duffgirl and Dufflad?
jokes.setup: Duffman pleading for his job
jokes.punchline: Names his children with Duff branding
jokes.explanation: Duffman's complete corporate identity extends to family naming. Shows how far the character integration goes.

jokes.text: I don't care if his name is Bony McDork. Just make the checks out to me.
jokes.setup: Contract negotiations for dog's new name
jokes.punchline: Homer's priorities are purely financial, regardless of dignity
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer greed overriding any other consideration. The made-up name 'Bony McDork' adds to the humor.

jokes.text: Very good. Now, let me stand on my mark between the snake and its baby...
jokes.setup: Krusty preparing for a TV segment with a dangerous snake
jokes.punchline: His terrible positioning choice
jokes.explanation: Typical Krusty poor decision-making. Sets up the physical comedy well.

jokes.text: How can you get a buzz chewing freeze-dried beer?
jokes.setup: Space mission is boring
jokes.punchline: Astronauts want alcohol but limited to freeze-dried versions
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about space food limitations. The specific complaint feels authentic to the situation.

jokes.text: How can you get a buzz chewing freeze-dried beer?
jokes.setup: Astronauts complaining about space
jokes.punchline: The specific complaint about freeze-dried beer
jokes.explanation: Decent observational humor about space food technology, but not particularly memorable.

jokes.text: National Association of the Sellers of Alcohol.
jokes.setup: NASA sponsorship announcement
jokes.punchline: NASA actually stands for alcohol sellers, not space agency
jokes.explanation: Clever misdirection using familiar acronym. The reveal recontextualizes the space setting as beer commercial.

jokes.text: Like eat at Arby's.
jokes.setup: Discussion about people doing crazy things in ads
jokes.punchline: Eating at Arby's presented as equally implausible
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy - three words that completely savage a restaurant chain. The deadpan delivery makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Kent, let me make this perfectly clear. I have no dog.
jokes.setup: News interview about Homer being cat person
jokes.punchline: Categorical denial of dog ownership while dog watches
jokes.explanation: Nixon-esque denial that becomes evidence against him. The formality of 'perfectly clear' makes the lie more dramatic.

jokes.text: Who's gonna eat my homework now?
jokes.setup: Family mourning the loss of their dog
jokes.punchline: Bart's uniquely selfish concern
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bart - finding the personal angle in every situation. Good character consistency.

jokes.text: If I keep talking here, Kelly can't jump in. Come on, Suds. Toss me a bone.
jokes.setup: TV host trying to interview a dog
jokes.punchline: Admits to camera strategy while asking dog for help with pun
jokes.explanation: Breaking fourth wall while being professional. The 'toss me a bone' pun acknowledges the absurdity of interviewing a dog.

jokes.text: That guy exploits his pets worse than Bob Guccione.
jokes.setup: Marge commenting on the dog's exploitation
jokes.punchline: Comparing it to the Penthouse magazine publisher
jokes.explanation: Dark reference that seems out of character for Marge. Doesn't quite land.

jokes.text: But show me a Simpson that isn't. / I'm not a coward. / [YELLING] / You're not a coward.
jokes.setup: Lisa claims all Simpsons are cowards, Bart objects
jokes.punchline: Bart immediately proves he's a coward by screaming
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup and immediate payoff. Bart's cowardice is demonstrated rather than described, making the joke stronger.

jokes.text: If you have 6 liters of blood and your blood is 80 percent alcohol...how much alcohol do you have? Anyone?
jokes.setup: Duffman teaching at rehabilitation center
jokes.punchline: Math word problem about lethal blood alcohol content
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about alcohol poisoning disguised as education. Duffman's inability to escape his beer persona is perfectly executed.

jokes.text: An icy-cold, full-bodied, beech wood-aged, amber-hued day in...
jokes.setup: Duffman saying 'cold day in hell' figure of speech
jokes.punchline: Can't help but describe the cold using beer marketing terms
jokes.explanation: Duffman's corporate programming overrides normal speech patterns. The beer descriptors are authentically marketing-speak.

jokes.text: Then I must be Jesus. Up, up and away!
jokes.setup: Someone says he's really Duffman
jokes.punchline: His illogical conclusion and Superman reference
jokes.explanation: Absurd logic chain typical of The Simpsons but not particularly memorable.

jokes.text: Mother, I served in Nam. / And you've been bitching about it for 30 years.
jokes.setup: Sea Captain calling someone a coward
jokes.punchline: Mother points out his constant complaints about war service
jokes.explanation: Good deflation of military bragging with family dynamics. The mother's bluntness and specificity of '30 years' work well.

jokes.text: Check it out. That shark's wasted. He's gone from predator to partier.
jokes.setup: Shark drinks beer and becomes drunk
jokes.punchline: Alliterative description of shark's transformation
jokes.explanation: The 'predator to partier' alliteration is clever and the concept of drunk shark is absurd enough to work.

jokes.text: Let me think. How did that beautiful young naked lady in Jaws make it out alive?
jokes.setup: Duffman trying to remember how to survive a shark attack
jokes.punchline: His confused memory of who survived Jaws
jokes.explanation: Movie reference humor but not particularly sharp or memorable.

jokes.text: Guys wanna be him, girls think they can change him.
jokes.setup: Crowd reaction to drunk shark
jokes.punchline: Applies dating psychology to dangerous marine predator
jokes.explanation: Excellent observation about gendered relationship patterns applied absurdly to shark. The psychology rings true despite the context.

jokes.text: All we have left are the earthquake ham and the condolence ham. / Marge, they're just hams, okay?
jokes.setup: Homer wants celebration ham but Marge has concerns about appropriate ham
jokes.punchline: Reveals extensive ham categorization system, Homer dismisses distinctions
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation of the ham system. The specificity of occasion-based hams and Homer's practical dismissal create perfect comedy.

jokes.text: To me, that says 'I love you.' / I think he left a 'big hug' in your lunchbox.
jokes.setup: Dog urinating on rug
jokes.punchline: Homer and Bart euphemistically describe dog's bathroom activities as affection
jokes.explanation: Homer's delusional positive spin on dog behavior. The euphemisms for bodily functions are appropriately silly.

jokes.text: All we have left are the earthquake ham and the condolence ham. / Marge, they're just hams, okay?
jokes.setup: Marge categorizing different emergency hams
jokes.punchline: Homer's exasperation at her system
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback to the emergency ham concept. Marge's categorization system vs Homer's practicality is perfect character work.

jokes.text: Everyone's against me.
jokes.setup: Family corrects Homer's false accusations against cat and dog
jokes.punchline: Homer's persecution complex when presented with facts
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer self-pity when reality conflicts with his narrative. Simple but effective character moment.

tmdb_overview: Homer throws out Santa's Little Helper for not saving him from a fire, but soon the dog becomes Duff's new mascot.
